channel.title: Aaron's notes
channel.description: Les dernières 40 notes on Aaron's notes

item.title: friendship
item.description: of pleasure. or utility? Friendship is cultivated over many interactions light and deep. Intimacy is earned through trust. Patricia’s on intimacy throughout friendship How does one weave that delicate fabric of trust, when so many are ensnared in their own lives, their hearts shuttered against vulnerability? To cultivate trust, we must first turn inward, nurturing the quiet confidence of self-trust before extending our hands to others.

item.title: Algebraic geometry
item.description: See also git source and web view.

item.title: Crib.
item.description: ¬(∃x∣R:P(x)) ¬(∀x∣R:P(x))​≡∀x∣R:¬P(x)≡∃x∣R:¬P(x)​ regular language δ^(q,ϵ) δ^(q,xa)​=q=δ(δ^(q,x),a)​ All finite languages are regular, but not all regular languages are ...

item.title: Monte-Carlo methods
item.description: tree search. a search algorithm based on random sampling of the search space. Selection: root R and select successive child nodes until leaf L is reached.

item.title: The Prisoner's Dilemma
item.description: a game theory thought experiment involves two rational agents, each of whom can cooperate for mutual benefit or “defect” for individual reward.

item.title: being.
item.description: What is being as a part of epistemology? why do we practice art? Practice anything, no matter how well or how bad, we practice art as a act of becoming, for our soul to grow. ...

item.title: game theory
item.description: emerged John von Neumann published the paper On the Theory of Games of Strategy, when Neumann’s original proff used Brower’s fixed-point theorem on continuous mappings into compact ...

item.title: Economics for engineer, a guide.
item.description: Economics for engineer, a guide.

item.title: Non-linear Optimization
item.description: See also slides.

item.title: Context-free grammar and push-down Turing machine
item.description: Problème 1. Give a context free grammar for the following language: L={anbmck∣k=n+m} Solution The following CFG generates the language L: SS1​S2​S3​ABC​→S1​∣S2​∣S3​→aS1​c∣aS1​∣A→bS2​c∣bS2​∣B→aS3​b∣cS3​∣C→aAc∣aA∣a∣ε→bBc∣bB∣b∣ε→aCb∣cC∣c∣ε​ Problème 2.

item.title: Observer and state-space model
item.description: Problemè 1 Consider the following state-space model: x˙y​=​00−5​10−6​010​​x+​001​​u=[1​0​0​]x​ Design an observer to place the observer poles at -10, -10, -15 Solution The ...

item.title: Steady state error and PID controller
item.description: See also problem Problemè 1 In Lab 4, We used a PD compensator to control our ball and beam apparatus. The transfer function of our PD compensator was as follows: GC​(s)=KD​s+KP​ However, we did not use the compensator in this form.

item.title: MST with shortest path optimisation
item.description: Problème 1. Consider we want to build McMaster Maps, the best online route planning tool in existence. The developers of McMaster maps have decided to represent road information in terms of a a massive graph in which nodes are crossing and the edges are the roads between crossings.

item.title: Joint-control open-loop system
item.description: See also problem Problemè 1 A robot arm has a joint-control open-loop transfer function G(s)=s(s+10)(s+40)300(s+100)​ 1.a Plot the asymptotic approximation of the Bode plot The ...

item.title: Attractor
item.description: A set of points described by a dynamical system. Some exhibits chaotic behaviour, see also Paul Bourke’s work Often create visually appealing patterns, but its applications range from physics to biology: how we understand weather patterns, bird migration patterns, quantum phenomena.

item.title: Shortest path and series-parallel graph
item.description: Problème 1. A regional government wants to improve their existing infrastructure between a collection of towns T. In specific, the government want to build a minimum number of roads such that there is a route from each town to each other town.

item.title: dundurn.
item.description: atelier with friends deux - orangeville.

item.title: Trees path
item.description: Problème 1. Let G=(N,E) be an undirected tree (graph G is undirected, connected, and has ∣N∣=∣E∣+1 if we count edges (v,w) and (w,v) as the same edge).

item.title: Uber compensation analysis
item.description: a Uber compensation policies case study.

item.title: Root locus and graphical analysis
item.description: See also problem Problemè 1 1.a What does a root locus plot depict? A root locus plot depicts locations of the closed-loop poles of a system in the complex s-plane as a function of a gain ...

item.title: distraction
item.description: See this tweet.

item.title: Regex, pumping lemma
item.description: Problème 1. Give regular expression for the languages bellow 1.a L={anbm∣(n+m)%2=0} L=(aa)∗(bb)∗+a(aa)∗b(bb)∗ 1.b L={w∣w does not contain the substring: aba} L=b∗+b∗a+b∗+b∗(ab+)∗ab∗ 1.c L={w∣w has an even number of b′s} L=a∗+a∗ba∗ba∗ Problème 2.

item.title: Open-loop system
item.description: See also problem Problemè 1 A unity feedback system has transfer function G(s)=s(s2+4s+13)K​ 1.a Plot the root locus for this problem We need to find the poles and zeros of the open-loop ...

item.title: Graph search and A-star
item.description: Problème 1. Consider a m×n game board in which each cell has a numeric value, e.g: GHIJK​A13123​B24433​C24112​D34341​E44114​F21422​​ A player starts the game with a token in the top-left cell (the cell GA in this example) and the player finishes the game by moving to the bottom-right cell (the cell KF in this example).

item.title: Color
item.description: theory. Complementary Analogous Triadic See also coolors.co contrast, combination, thickness background Color surface area .

item.title: Gestalt Principles
item.description: Relates to how we perceive composition Proximity Common Region.

item.title: composition
item.description: How we combine elements to make a comprehensive model. Uses Color.

item.title: design
item.description: what?.

item.title: orangeville.
item.description: atelier with friends uno - orangeville.

item.title: Automata
item.description: Book: pdf Q1: T/F, if F explain why. Q4: regular expression, 5 separate q Q2/Q3: DFAs and NFAs Product construction: ∩ ∪ of DFAs Subset construction: NFA to DFA Quotient construction: State minimization Set theory Complement in Σ∗: L=Σ∗−L associative: (A∪B)∪C(A∩B)∩C(AB)C​=A∪(B∪C),=A∩(B∩C),=A(BC).​ commutative: A∪BA∩B​=B∪A=B∩A​ null set null set ∅ is the identity for ∪ and annihilator for set concatenation A∪∅=A and A∅=∅A=∅ set {ϵ} is an identity for set concatenation {ϵ}A=A{ϵ}=A Set union and intersection are distributive over set concatenation A∪(B∩C) A∩(B∪C)​=(A∪B)∩(A∪C)=(A∩B)∪(A∩C)​ Set concatenation distributes over union A(B∪C) (A∪B)C​=AB∪AC=AC∪BC​ DFA DFA#definition Let δ:Q×Σ→Q, thus δ^:Q×Σ∗→Q δ(q,c)→p therefore δ^(q,w)→p regularity Important δ^(q,ϵ) δ^(q,xa)​=q=δ(δ^(q,x),a)​ Important a subset A⊂Σ∗ is regular if and only if there exists a DFA M such that L(M)=L Important All finite languages are regular, but not all regular languages are finite examples Show L is regular where L={x∣x%3=0∪x=ϵ}, with Σ={0,1} Three states, q0​,q1​,q2​, where q0​ denotes the string mod 3 is 0, q1​ denotes the string mod 3 is 1, and q2​ denotes the string mod 3 is 2.

item.title: atelier with friends.
item.description: Somewhat impromptu supper club hosted by yours truly. See also dishes for a comprehensive repertoire.

item.title: Atmospheric properties for exoplanets
item.description: Ref: slides features. H=ωmH​gkB​T​ solid or dash solid q larger mean molecular weights? B: shallow features ⇒ higher mean molecular weights mass-metallicity trend Question Can we detect clouds in exoplanets? Clouds suppress atmospheric chemical signatures Important Introduced a degeneracy between cloud-top pressure and mean molecular weight clouds/winds on giant planets.

item.title: steady-state error
item.description: See also slides Important System type is the number of integrators in the forward path, thus value of n .

item.title: Alignment
item.description: See also: Overton Window and this blog on alignment research The act of aligning oneself with a particular group or ideology. This can be done for a variety of reasons, including: To gain social acceptance To gain power To gain resources Often known as a solution to solve “hallucination” in large models token-generation.

item.title: Overton Window
item.description: also known as window of discourse A window into the ideas that frames ideas that people are prepared to entertain. All ideas outside the window are not seriously considered.

item.title: ethics
item.description: Closely connected to value theory, or moral philosophy. Kantian ethics presupposes that there is a universal moral law that applies to all rational beings, or his deontological ethics framework that based on “categorical imperative”.

item.title: Root mean square
item.description: See also: pdf problème 1. The Root Mean Square (RMS) value of a signal f(t) that is periodic with period T is given by the equation T1​∫0T​(f(t))2dt​ It can be shown that the RMS value of u(t)=Bsinωt is 2​B​ 1.a Square wave The square wave function is defined as: f(t)={10​if 0≤t<2T​if 2T​≤t<T​ import sympy as sp t = sp.symbols('t') T = 2 RMS = sp.sqrt(1/T * sp.integrate(1, (t, 0, T/2))) RMS = 2​1​ 1.b Sawtooth wave A sawtooth wave function is defined as: f(t)=T2A​(t−2T​) import sympy as sp t = sp.symbols('t') T = 1 A = 0.5 f_t = 2 * A / T * (t - T/2) RMS = sp.sqrt(1/T * sp.integrate(f_t**2, (t, 0, T))) RMS = 63​​ 1.c sine wave A general form of the sine wave can be written as f(t)=Asin(ωt+ϕ) Amplitude is 2.3, no phase shift RMS = 2​2.3​ problème 2.

item.title: PJRT
item.description: Blog post and source Lower stack layer for framework and hardware communication. As abstraction to transpile to different hardware targets: TPU, GPU.

item.title: TPU
item.description: See also: XLA, and architecture.
